semantic_description: Verifies string_collapse_spaces, which scans a null-terminated input character array s of logical length n and writes to caller-provided output buffer out while collapsing each maximal run of ASCII space characters 32 into a single 32. The loop is a while(1) scan with index i over the input and index j over the output, maintains an in_space flag for whether the previous emitted/seen class is a space run, preserves the input buffer, writes a null terminator at out[j], and leaves the remaining output buffer suffix unconstrained by the postcondition witness.
